summary: Tested the TikTok Auto-Live detector rewrite (_is_tiktok_user_live), the new /api/debug/tiktok-live/{handle} endpoint, and regression on live-sub create/my/cancel. 11/12 pytest checks pass. CRITICAL finding: signal-2 (HTML scrape) triggers a FALSE POSITIVE for essentially every handle — the substrings 'liveroominfo' and 'roomid":' appear in the /@handle/live SSR HTML for ALL users (live OR offline OR nonexistent) because those are JSON schema KEY NAMES, not runtime values. As a result the detector currently reports is_live=True for a nonsense handle '_this_is_definitely_not_a_real_handle_xyz123_' and for offline accounts like 'tiktok'/'nba'/'zachking'/'charlidamelio'. This flips the original bug on its head: instead of never firing orders, the worker will now fire orders for EVERY subscription on EVERY repeat_every_minutes tick — even for handles that are offline or don't exist — draining user balance.

critical.issue: HTML signal-2 marker set contains substrings that appear in TikTok /@handle/live SSR HTML for ALL users regardless of live status. Confirmed via direct httpx probe against tiktok/nba/zachking/charlidamelio and the nonsense handle — every one has 'liveroominfo' + 'roomid":' present (schema keys, not values), which is >=2 markers => detector returns True. Off-markers ('usernotlive','"userstatus":1','"islive":false') do NOT appear in any of these responses either, so the guard doesn't rescue it. Net effect: detector returns True universally → worker will burst-order for every subscription regardless of actual live state, draining users' wallets. This is a REGRESSION worse than the original bug.

For non-live handles the endpoint returns HTTP 200 with body {data:{}} (no user/room/liveRoomInfo keys). Current code correctly falls through to signal 2 here, but that means signal-1 provides no coverage — the entire detection currently relies on the broken signal 2.

minor.issue: Endpoint returns 200 with correct shape {handle, is_live: bool}. Auth-gated correctly (401 without token). Works for @-prefixed input. However the returned is_live is wrong for all tested handles — see critical entry.

action_items: CRITICAL: Redesign _is_tiktok_user_live() signal 2. The markers 'liveroominfo' and 'roomid":' are schema keys always present in TikTok's SSR HTML and MUST NOT be treated as positive live indicators. Suggested approach: (a) rely primarily on the webcast info_by_scene API — treat empty data:{} as NOT live and only True when data.user.status==2 OR data.liveRoomInfo.liveRoomId truthy AND data.liveRoomInfo.status==2; (b) for HTML fallback use value-based patterns like '"liveRoomInfo":{"liveRoomId":"' followed by digits (regex \"liveRoomId\":\"\d+\") together with '"status":2' or 'is_live":true'; (c) additionally check the webcast endpoint's /api/live/detail/ or the newer /webcast/user/get_by_uid/ — signature: {data:{user:{room_id: <non-zero-int>, status:2}}}; (d) unit-test with 4-5 known-offline handles that MUST return False.
Add a smoke test on backend startup that probes _is_tiktok_user_live('tiktok') and logs a warning if it returns True (since tiktok is rarely live), to catch future regressions.
Consider gating any burst order behind a second confirmation call (e.g. hit /@handle/live again after 10s) to reduce false-positive spend risk once the detector is fixed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:2216-2226 — substring matching on lowered SSR HTML is inherently fragile; TikTok ships JSON schema keys in SSR that will always match these substrings. Use regex on values instead of substrings on keys.
server.py:2402 — worker sleep is 30s but next_check_at default is 60s; fine but means every sub is polled twice per minute if under load — acceptable.

server.py:2246-2270 — no upper bound on quantity_per_burst * duration_days combined spend; consider adding a hard daily cap so a runaway detector can't drain wallet in minutes. Especially relevant given the current detector bug.

success_rate.backend: 92% (11/12 pytest tests pass) — the failing test is the false-positive detection assertion, which is a REAL bug not a test flake

test_credentials: testbugfix1 / password1 (auto_live_enabled + $50 wallet ensured); admin secret haha123.
seed_data_creation: Inserted synthetic active live-subscription docs (id prefix TEST-iter27-*) directly via pymongo; all cleaned up in fixture teardown. Also toggled auto_live_enabled=true and adjusted-balance +$50 for testbugfix1 for regression tests (not reverted — acceptable persistent state for a test user).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_iter27_live_sub_bugfix.py. When main agent fixes _is_tiktok_user_live, re-run the whole file — the currently-failing TestDebugTiktokLiveEndpoint::test_debug_nonexistent_handle_no_500 MUST pass (nonsense handle → is_live=false). Also consider adding an assertion that 'tiktok'/'nba' typically return False (they're rarely live). To run: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB_NAME=test_database python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iter27_live_sub_bugfix.py -v`. Worker liveness test passed — worker DOES pick up due subs and updates last_check_at within ~30s.
rca of the issue: Original bug: _is_tiktok_user_live() always False → worker never fired. Rewrite added webcast API + HTML multi-marker fallback. Rewrite's HTML fallback uses substrings that are JSON KEY NAMES ('liveroominfo', 'roomid":') always present in TikTok's SSR HTML shell, so signal 2 returns True universally. Webcast API returns empty data:{} for non-live users (returns False from signal 1, then falls through to broken signal 2). Reproduction: `curl 'https://www.tiktok.com/@ANY_HANDLE/live' | tr A-Z a-z | grep -c 'liveroominfo\|roomid"'` returns ≥2 for every handle tested. Mitigation: switch signal 2 to VALUE-based matching (regex on liveRoomId numeric value, is_live:true after a colon, etc.) and require an explicit positive value marker rather than schema-key presence.
